channel.title: Page on Luiyología
channel.description: Recent content in Page on Luiyología

item.title: Main public talks
item.description:
List of the main public talks I have humbly given (in reverse chronological order)

Title Details Presentation
Tolkien & GenAI XXVIII Mereth Aldaron Enyalië
(Altafulla, Spain)
5/DEC/2024

Source Code in GitHub
Concerning Governance in Programming Languages, 2020 edition Congreso esLibre 2020
(Online Conference)
18/SEP/2020

Source Code in GitHub
Link to video
Concerning Governance in Programming Languages, 2019 edition Commit Conf
(Madrid, Spain)
23/NOV/2019

Source Code in GitHub
Link to video
Design principles for an event driven architecture Commit Conf
(Madrid, Spain)
22/NOV/2019

Link to video
Omni-Channel Customer-centric strategies in a modern architecture Big Things Conference
(Madrid, Spain)
21/NOV/2019

Link to video
Design principles for an event driven architecture Apache Kafka Meetup
(Madrid, Spain)
24/OCT/2019
Measuring Health and Ethics in Programming Languages FLOSSCommunityMetrics
(Brussels, Belgium)
29/JAN/2016

Source Code in GitHub
Concerning Governance in Programming Languages Codemotion 2015
(Madrid, Spain)
27/NOV/2015

Source Code in GitHub
Technical Debt for developers… ¡and managers! Codemotion 2015
(Madrid, Spain)
27/NOV/2015

Source Code in GitHub
OpenStreetMap project and community: open, collaborative and free OpenExpoDay 2015
(Madrid, Spain)
16/JUN/2015

Source Code in GitHub

item.title: My Curriculum Vitae as a traveler
item.description:
(Inspired by the CV Viajero by Dendarii)

Visited Countries

I have visited 25 out of 193 countries (among those recognized by United Nations plus the observer states). This is the list in alphabetical order:

Country Main visited places (x times) Visiting order
Algeria Ahaggar National Park, Argel, Djanet, Tamanrasset, Tassili N’Ajjer National Park 25th
Andorra Andorra la Vella 2nd
Austria Romantikstrasse & Vienna 15th
Belgium Bruges, Brussels (x7) & Ghent 13th
China Beijing & Great Wall 20th
Czech Republic Kutná Hora & Prague (x4) 14th
France Annecy, Auray, Bayeaux, Bordeaux, Caen, Cancale, Carcassonne, Carnac, Concarneau, Dinard, Euro Disney, Fougeres, Grenoble, Honfleur, La Loire à Vélo, Le Mans, Mont Sant Michel, Morlaix, Paris (x3), Pointe du Raz, Pont-Aven, Quimper, Rennes, Rochefort-en-Terre, Rouen, Saint Malo, Tolouse, Vannes & Vitré 10th
Germany Aachen, Augsburg, Bad Mergentheim, Berlin, Bonn, Cologne, Dachau, Dinkelsbühl, Donauwörth, Düsseldorf, Feuchtwangen, Frankfurt am Main, Füssen, Landsberg am Lech, Munich, Nördlingen, Nuremberg, Potsdam, Rothenburg ob der Tauber, Röttingen, Schwangau, Tauberbischofsheim, Weikersheim & Würzburg 3rd
Hungary Budapest 17th
Iceland Blue Lagoon, Dettifoss, Húsavik, Jökulsárlón, Kvernufoss, Landmannalaugar, Reykholt, Reykjavík, Selfoss, Seljalandsfoss, Seydisfjordur, Skaftafell, Skogafoss, Studlagil, Thingvellir, Thorsmork & Vik 23rd
Ireland Connemara, Dublín & Galway 9th
Italy Milan (x2) & Rome 4th
Japan Hiroshima, Itsukushima, Kyoto, Nagoya, Osaka, Shirakawa-go, Takayama & Tokyo 22nd
Netherlands Amsterdam (x12), Edam, Haarlem (x3), Marken, Noordwijk, Rotterdam (x2), Utrecht & Volendam 6th
Poland Austchwitz, Katowice (x2), Krakow, Wieliczka, Wroclaw & Zakopane 18th
Portugal Amarante, Braga, Bragança, Évora, Guimarães, Lisboa, Porto, Póvoa de Varzim, Sintra, Tavira, Vila Real & Vila Real de Santo Antonio 8th
Romania Bucharest 19th
Serbia Belrade 24th
Slovakia Bratislava 16th
Spain too many 1st
Switzerland Aareschlucht, Almendhubel, CERN, Chillon, Geneve, Gruyeres, Interlaken, Jungfraujoch, Lausanne, Meiringen, Montreux, Mürren & Reichenbackfall 12th
United Kingdom Balloch, Bath, Birmingham, Blenheim Palace, Bristol, Cambridge, Cawdor, Cromarty, Durham, Edinburgh, Glasgow, Glencoe Valley, Inverness, Isle of Skye, Loch Ness, London (x5), Luss, Manchester, Newcastle upon Tyne, Oban, Oxford (x2), Pitlochry, Stirling, Stonehenge & Taunton (x3) 7th
United Republic of Tanzania Tarangire National Park, Serengeti National Park & Ngorongoro Conservation Area 21st
United States New York & Washington D.C. 11th
Vatican City State Vatican City 5th

In chronological order

2026

  • MAR

    Frankfurt am Main (Germany)

  • JAN

    Belgium

    • Ghent, Bruges, Brussels, FOSDEM 2026
  • JAN

    Tenerife

    • Santa Cruz de Tenerife, Candelaria, San Cristóbal de La Laguna, Macizo de Anaga, Puerto de la Cruz, Masca, Los Gigantes, Puerto de Santiago, Playa de los Cristianos, El Médano, Vilaflor, Teno Rural Park and Teide National Park

2025

  • SEP

    Parc National des Pyrénees and Pays Basque (France)

    • Gavarnie, Pont d’Espagne, Capbreton, Bayonne, Biarritz, Saint-Jean-de-Luz
  • AUG

    Tuscany (Italy)

    • Bologna, Firenze, Lucca, Pisa, Pistoia, Cinque Terre, Castellina in Chianti, Pienza, Montepulciano, Siena, San Gimignano
  • MAY

    Current London 2025 - London (United Kingdom)

  • MAY

    Zona Lúdica 2025 - Málaga (Spain)

  • ABR

    Algeria

    • Argel, Tamanrasset, Ahaggar National Park, Tassili N’Ajjer NAtional Park, Djanet
  • MAR

    Belgrade, Serbia

  • MAR

    Amsterdam (Netherlands)

2024

  • NOV

    Amsterdam (Netherlands)

  • NOV

    Huesca

    • Valle de Pineta, Bielsa, Escuaín, Añisclo, Punta Mondoto, Ainsa
  • OCT

    Amsterdam (Netherlands)

  • SEP

    Iceland

    • Selfoss, Skogafoss, Thorsmork, Seljalandsfoss, Kvernufoss, Vik, Skaftafell, Jökulsárlón, Seydisfjordur, Studlagil, Dettifoss, Húsavik, Reykholt, Reykjavík, Blue Lagoon, Thingvellir, Landmannalaugar
  • AUG

    Galicia (Spain)

    • Ribadeo, Foz, Sargadelos, San Cibrao, Souto da Retorta, Fragas do Eume, A Coruña, Lugo
  • AUG

    Tierra de Nadie 2024 - Málaga (Spain)

  • JUN

item.title: About the author
item.description:
I am a Computer Engineering graduate, with strong experience guiding and coordinating high performance teams in strategic projects. My skills (emphasizing leadership, mentoring, organization and analysis) have led me to perform all kinds of tasks in addition to the purely technical.

I first learned how to code in the 1990s (developing simple games in BASIC) but it was around 2008 when I started living on it first as a software engineer but very soon also as technical leader, team manager or software architect (usually with more than one role at the same time). ING crossed my path in 2010 and since then I have been involved in different critical projects where I have performed key roles in the Architecture department as Enterprise Architect and Information Architecture Expert Lead but also other roles more related to tech delivery such as IT Area Lead or IT Lead (Technology Director) for the entire COO Domain.

Having a strong interest and experience with Open Source, I am an active participant in several worldwide associations and communities. I still love to learn and also love to teach, so I not only attend but also give as many talks and trainings as I can to contribute back to the community.

My great passion is traveling, so on this website you can find my curriculum vitae as a traveler and many posts with chronicles about my most notable trips. I write them mainly for myself, but I hope they can be useful for any visitor.

I’m an active fellow of several national and international associations: ARP-SAPC (promoting critical thinking), STE (Sociedad Tolkien Española) and the Tolkien Society.

I’m also Member of Free Software Foundation Europe, OpenStreetMap Foundation, Spanish Red Cross, Doctors Without Borders, Amnesty International and UNHCR, to name a few.

I love hiking, cycling (cycle tourism specially) and traditional archery to the point that I’m federated in FMM (Federación Madrileña de Montañismo) and RFETA (Spanish Federation of Archery) and I’ve co-founded with other friends our own traditional archery club: the Archers of Ithilien.
